<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead">
## Task: flt-it8.32.16 — Secrets and dependency scanning

### Issue Details
Integrate secret scanning and dependency vulnerability scanning with documented artifact handling for CI.

### Deliverables (from plan)
- `gitleaks` integration
- Either `OSV-Scanner` or `Trivy` for dependency scanning
- CI documentation and artifact plan

### Current State
`scripts/ci/run-static-analyzers.sh` exists with gosec, govulncheck, and semgrep sections. It needs gitleaks and dependency scanning sections added.

What's needed:
1. Add `gitleaks` section to `scripts/ci/run-static-analyzers.sh` — same pattern as existing tools (check if installed, run, report findings)
2. Add `osv-scanner` (or `trivy`) section to `scripts/ci/run-static-analyzers.sh`
3. A `make scan-secrets` Makefile target for running gitleaks standalone
4. A `make scan-deps` Makefile target for running dependency scanning standalone
5. Documentation in `docs/ci/` about the scanning tools, installation, and artifact handling

### IMPORTANT: Follow CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md conventions
- Read CLAUDE.md at the repo root before starting
- Keep changes minimal — don't over-engineer
- Same graceful-skip pattern as existing analyzers (skip if not installed)

### Quality Gates
Since this only touches CI/scripts files (no Go or web source):
```
make api-test-unit && make api-lint
```

## WORKTREE SETUP — RUN FIRST
Before doing anything else, create and enter an isolated worktree:
```bash
REPO_ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"
BRANCH_NAME="worktree/secrets-scanning"
WORKTREE_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/.claude/worktrees/$BRANCH_NAME"

if [ -d "$WORKTREE_DIR" ]; then
  echo "Worktree already exists, reusing"
else
  git worktree add "$WORKTREE_DIR" -b "$BRANCH_NAME"
fi

cd "$WORKTREE_DIR"
```
Verify you are in the right place:
```bash
echo "CWD: $(pwd)"
echo "Branch: $(git branch --show-current)"
echo "$(pwd)" | grep -q '\.claude/worktrees/' && echo "WORKTREE VERIFIED" || echo "FATAL: NOT in a worktree"
```
If verification fails, STOP and report the error to the team lead.

ALL subsequent commands must run from this worktree directory.
NEVER cd back to the main repo directory. Commit and push ONLY on
your worktree branch — never touch main.

When done, push your branch:
```bash
git push -u origin HEAD
```

</teammate-message>

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Run available static analyzers, skip gracefully if not installed.
# Exit code: 0 = no findings (or no tools installed), 1 = findings reported.
set -euo pipefail

repo_root="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"
cd "$repo_root"

FINDINGS=0
RAN=0

# --- gosec ---
if command -v gosec &>/dev/null; then
    echo "=== gosec ==="
    if (cd api && gosec ./...); then
        echo "gosec: clean"
    else
        echo "gosec: findings reported"
        FINDINGS=1
    fi
    RAN=1
    echo ""
else
    echo "gosec not installed — skipping"
fi

# --- govulncheck ---
if command -v govulncheck &>/dev/null; then
    echo "=== govulncheck ==="
    if (cd api && govulncheck ./...); then
        echo "govulncheck: clean"
    else
        echo "govulncheck: findings reported"
        FINDINGS=1
    fi
    RAN=1
    echo ""
else
    echo "govulncheck not installed — skipping"
fi

# --- semgrep ---
if command -v semgrep &>/dev/null; then
    echo "=== semgrep ==="
    if semgrep --config auto --quiet .; then
        echo "semgrep: clean"
    else
        echo "semgrep: findings reported"
        FINDINGS=1
    fi
    RAN=1
    echo ""
else
    echo "semgrep not installed — skipping"
fi

if [[ "$RAN" == "0" ]]; then
    echo "No static analyzers installed. Install gosec, govulncheck, or semgrep for enhanced checks."
fi

exit "$FINDINGS"

## Flotsam Monorepo Makefile — delegates to component Makefiles
## Usage: make <target>

# Load .env if present — makes vars available to shell commands in recipes
-include .env

.PHONY: api-build api-run api-generate api-test api-test-unit api-test-tdd \
        api-lint api-migrate-up api-migrate-down api-clean \
        web-install web-dev web-build web-lint web-clean web-test web-test-tdd web-schema-sync \
        lint \
        dev dev-db-up dev-db-down \
        test-quick test-standard test-full \
        ci-changed ci-full hooks-install \
        help

## api-build: Build all API binaries (flotsamd, flotsam-worker, flotsam)
api-build:
	$(MAKE) -C api build

## api-run: Run the API server (flotsamd)
api-run:
	$(MAKE) -C api run

## api-run-worker: Run the job worker (flotsam-worker --watch)
api-run-worker:
	$(MAKE) -C api run-worker

## api-generate: Run GraphQL code generation
api-generate:
	$(MAKE) -C api generate

## api-test: Run all API tests
api-test:
	$(MAKE) -C api test

## api-test-unit: Run API unit tests
api-test-unit:
	$(MAKE) -C api test-unit

## api-test-tdd: Run API tests with tdd-guard-go when installed
api-test-tdd:
	$(MAKE) -C api test-tdd

## api-lint: Run linter on the API
api-lint:
	$(MAKE) -C api lint

## api-migrate-up: Apply database migrations
api-migrate-up:
	$(MAKE) -C api migrate-up

## api-migrate-down: Roll back last database migration
api-migrate-down:
	$(MAKE) -C api migrate-down

## api-seed: Populate dev database with sample data
api-seed:
	$(MAKE) -C api seed

## api-dev-token: Mint a dev JWT for local testing
api-dev-token:
	$(MAKE) -C api dev-token

## api-clean: Remove API build artifacts
api-clean:
	$(MAKE) -C api clean

## web-install: Install web frontend dependencies
web-install:
	$(MAKE) -C web install

## web-dev: Start Vite dev server (port 8080, proxies /graphql → :8081)
web-dev:
	$(MAKE) -C web dev

## web-build: Type-check and build web frontend for production
web-build:
	$(MAKE) -C web build

## web-lint: Run ESLint on web frontend
web-lint:
	$(MAKE) -C web lint

## web-clean: Remove web build artifacts and node_modules
web-clean:
	$(MAKE) -C web clean

## web-test: Run web unit tests
web-test:
	$(MAKE) -C web test

## web-test-tdd: Run web tests with TDD Guard reporter when configured
web-test-tdd:
	$(MAKE) -C web test-tdd

## web-schema-sync: Regenerate web/schema.graphql and graphql-env.d.ts from API SDL files
web-schema-sync:
	cat api/graph/schema/*.graphql > web/schema.graphql
	cd web && pnpm exec gql-tada generate-output --tsconfig tsconfig.app.json

## lint: Run all linters (Go + web)
lint: api-lint web-lint

## dev: Start full-stack dev environment (Docker PG + air + Vite) with hot reload
dev:
	bash scripts/dev.sh

## dev-db-up: Start standalone dev database (PG 16 + pgvector + MinIO on port 5433)
dev-db-up:
	docker compose -f compose/db.yml up -d --wait
	@echo ""
	@echo "Dev database starting on port 5433. Use:"
	@echo "  DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/flotsam?sslmode=disable"
	@echo ""
	@echo "MinIO console: http://localhost:9001 (minioadmin/minioadmin)"

## dev-db-down: Stop and remove the standalone dev database container
dev-db-down:
	docker compose -f compose/db.yml down

## test-quick: Fast checks — Go unit tests, TS type-check + build, ESLint (~10-20s, no DB)
test-quick: api-test-unit web-build web-lint

## test-standard: Full local validation — quick + web unit tests + Go lint (~30-60s, no DB)
test-standard: test-quick web-test api-lint

## test-full: Complete validation — standard + integration tests (requires DB + running services)
test-full: test-standard api-test

## ci-changed: Run risk-aware checks on changed files (vs origin/main)
ci-changed:
	bash scripts/ci/run-changed.sh

## ci-full: Run full-repo policy and analyzer checks
ci-full:
	bash scripts/ci/run-full.sh

## hooks-install: Configure git to use .githooks/ for pre-commit and pre-push
hooks-install:
	bash scripts/install-hooks.sh

## help: List all available targets
help:
	@echo ""
	@echo "Usage: make <target>"
	@echo ""
	@grep -hE '^## [a-z]' $(MAKEFILE_LIST) | sed 's/## //' | sort -t: -k1,1 | \
		while IFS=: read -r target desc; do \
			printf "  %-24s %s\n" "$$target" "$$desc"; \
		done

1→# Generic CI Blueprint
     2→
     3→This repository now includes generic CI entrypoints that are independent of any
     4→specific CI vendor:
     5→
     6→- `make ci-changed`
     7→- `make ci-full`
     8→- `make hooks-install`
     9→- `make api-test-tdd`
    10→- `make web-test-tdd`
    11→
    12→## Design Goals
    13→
    14→- Keep CI vendor-neutral until a platform is chosen
    15→- Reuse the same scripts locally, in git hooks, and in CI
    16→- Make current debt visible without forcing immediate repo-wide breakage
    17→- Allow strict enforcement later by flipping environment variables
    18→
    19→## Current Modes
    20→
    21→The CI scripts support two control flags:
    22→
    23→- `FLOTSAM_STRICT=1`
    24→  - Missing analyzers or failed policy checks become fatal
    25→- `FLOTSAM_RUN_QUALITY_GATES=1`
    26→  - Execute project quality gates in addition to policy/analyzer checks
    27→
    28→Without those flags, the scripts run in audit mode and report issues without
    29→failing by default.
    30→
    31→## Script Layout
    32→
    33→- `scripts/ci/detect-risk.py`
    34→  - Classifies changed files against `policy/risk-map.json`
    35→- `scripts/ci/check-doc-commands.sh`
    36→  - Audits docs for missing scripts, missing root `make` targets, and stale
    37→    `bd` commands
    38→- `scripts/ci/check-placeholders.py`
    39→  - Audits docs that describe shipped features while code still exposes
    40→    placeholder handlers
    41→- `scripts/ci/run-static-analyzers.sh`
    42→  - Runs optional analyzers if installed
    43→- `scripts/ci/run-changed.sh`
    44→  - For local hooks and pre-merge CI on diffs
    45→- `scripts/ci/run-full.sh`
    46→  - For scheduled or branch-level full repo CI
    47→
    48→## Analyzer Rollout
    49→
    50→Recommended open source analyzers:
    51→
    52→- `Semgrep`
    53→  - Cross-language custom rules for Go, TypeScript, Kotlin, and repo policies
    54→- `gosec`
    55→  - Go security bug patterns
    56→- `govulncheck`
    57→  - Reachable Go dependency vulnerabilities
    58→- `gitleaks`
    59→  - Secret scanning
    60→- `Android lint`
    61→  - First-party Android static analysis
    62→- `detekt`
    63→  - Kotlin static analysis once added to Gradle
    64→- `OSV-Scanner`
    65→  - Future dependency scanning for `go.mod`, `pnpm-lock.yaml`, and Gradle
    66→- `Trivy`
    67→  - Optional future repo-wide vulnerability/secret/misconfig scanning
    68→
    69→The current scripts directly support:
    70→
    71→- `gitleaks`
    72→- `gosec`
    73→- `govulncheck`
    74→- `Android lint`
    75→- `detekt` when configured
    76→- local Semgrep rules when added under `policy/semgrep/`
    77→
    78→## TDD Guard
    79→
    80→The repo now includes shared Claude hook config in
    81→[.claude/settings.json](/home/ketan/project/flotsam/.claude/settings.json) and a
    82→wrapper script at
    83→[scripts/hooks/claude-tdd-guard.sh](/home/ketan/project/flotsam/scripts/hooks/claude-tdd-guard.sh).
    84→
    85→### Install
    86→
    87→CLI:
    88→
    89→```bash
    90→npm install -g tdd-guard
    91→```
    92→
    93→Go reporter:
    94→
    95→```bash
    96→go install github.com/nizos/tdd-guard/reporters/go/cmd/tdd-guard-go@latest
    97→```
    98→
    99→Vitest reporter:
   100→
   101→```bash
   102→cd web
   103→pnpm add -D tdd-guard-vitest
   104→```
   105→
   106→### Make targets
   107→
   108→- `make api-test-tdd`
   109→  - runs `go test -json` and pipes to `tdd-guard-go` when installed
   110→- `make web-test-tdd`
   111→  - runs Vitest with `TDD_GUARD_PROJECT_ROOT` set
   112→
   113→## CI Implementation Steps
   114→
   115→When a CI platform is chosen:
   116→
   117→1. Run `make ci-changed` on pull requests
   118→2. Run `make ci-full` on protected branches and scheduled jobs
   119→3. Set:
   120→   - `FLOTSAM_STRICT=1`
   121→   - `FLOTSAM_RUN_QUALITY_GATES=1`
   122→4. Install analyzers in the CI environment
   123→5. Add artifacts for:
   124→   - coverage reports
   125→   - analyzer SARIF or text output
   126→   - hook script logs if needed
   127→
   128→## Coverage Guidance
   129→
   130→- Web already defines Vitest coverage thresholds; `make ci-full` is wired to use
   131→  `pnpm test:coverage` once `FLOTSAM_RUN_QUALITY_GATES=1`.
   132→- Go coverage thresholds should be added as a follow-up once the preferred
   133→  reporting format and enforcement target are chosen.
   134→- Android and extension coverage thresholds should wait until those test suites
   135→  are substantial enough to avoid meaningless numbers.
   136→
   137→## Example Generic Stages
   138→
   139→1. `setup`
   140→   - install Go, Node, Java
   141→   - install analyzer binaries
   142→2. `policy`
   143→   - `make ci-changed` or `make ci-full`
   144→3. `quality`
   145→   - full quality gates with `FLOTSAM_RUN_QUALITY_GATES=1`
   146→4. `security`
   147→   - Semgrep, gosec, govulncheck, gitleaks, Android lint, detekt
   148→5. `artifacts`
   149→   - publish coverage and analyzer output
   150→
   151→## Notes
   152→
   153→- The repo is not yet ready for strict enforcement across every surface.
   154→- The scripts are intentionally audit-first until the existing backlog is
   155→  reduced.
   156→- Once the current governance debt is addressed, audit mode should be retired
   157→  in CI.
   158→

- `make ci-changed`
- `make ci-full`
- `make hooks-install`
- `make api-test-tdd`
- `make web-test-tdd`

## Design Goals

- Keep CI vendor-neutral until a platform is chosen
- Reuse the same scripts locally, in git hooks, and in CI
- Make current debt visible without forcing immediate repo-wide breakage
- Allow strict enforcement later by flipping environment variables

## Current Modes

The CI scripts support two control flags:

- `FLOTSAM_STRICT=1`
  - Missing analyzers or failed policy checks become fatal
- `FLOTSAM_RUN_QUALITY_GATES=1`
  - Execute project quality gates in addition to policy/analyzer checks

Without those flags, the scripts run in audit mode and report issues without
failing by default.

## Script Layout

- `scripts/ci/detect-risk.py`
  - Classifies changed files against `policy/risk-map.json`
- `scripts/ci/check-doc-commands.sh`
  - Audits docs for missing scripts, missing root `make` targets, and stale
    `bd` commands
- `scripts/ci/check-placeholders.py`
  - Audits docs that describe shipped features while code still exposes
    placeholder handlers
- `scripts/ci/run-static-analyzers.sh`
  - Runs optional analyzers if installed
- `scripts/ci/run-changed.sh`
  - For local hooks and pre-merge CI on diffs
- `scripts/ci/run-full.sh`
  - For scheduled or branch-level full repo CI

## Analyzer Rollout

Recommended open source analyzers:

- `Semgrep`
  - Cross-language custom rules for Go, TypeScript, Kotlin, and repo policies
- `gosec`
  - Go security bug patterns
- `govulncheck`
  - Reachable Go dependency vulnerabilities
- `gitleaks`
  - Secret scanning
- `Android lint`
  - First-party Android static analysis
- `detekt`
  - Kotlin static analysis once added to Gradle
- `OSV-Scanner`
  - Future dependency scanning for `go.mod`, `pnpm-lock.yaml`, and Gradle
- `Trivy`
  - Optional future repo-wide vulnerability/secret/misconfig scanning

The current scripts directly support:

- `gitleaks`
- `gosec`
- `govulncheck`
- `Android lint`
- `detekt` when configured
- local Semgrep rules when added under `policy/semgrep/`

## TDD Guard

The repo now includes shared Claude hook config in
[.claude/settings.json](/home/ketan/project/flotsam/.claude/settings.json) and a
wrapper script at
[scripts/hooks/claude-tdd-guard.sh](/home/ketan/project/flotsam/scripts/hooks/claude-tdd-guard.sh).

### Install

CLI:

```bash
npm install -g tdd-guard
```

Go reporter:

```bash
go install github.com/nizos/tdd-guard/reporters/go/cmd/tdd-guard-go@latest
```

Vitest reporter:

```bash
cd web
pnpm add -D tdd-guard-vitest
```

### Make targets

- `make api-test-tdd`
  - runs `go test -json` and pipes to `tdd-guard-go` when installed
- `make web-test-tdd`
  - runs Vitest with `TDD_GUARD_PROJECT_ROOT` set

## CI Implementation Steps

When a CI platform is chosen:

1. Run `make ci-changed` on pull requests
2. Run `make ci-full` on protected branches and scheduled jobs
3. Set:
   - `FLOTSAM_STRICT=1`
   - `FLOTSAM_RUN_QUALITY_GATES=1`
4. Install analyzers in the CI environment
5. Add artifacts for:
   - coverage reports
   - analyzer SARIF or text output
   - hook script logs if needed

## Coverage Guidance

- Web already defines Vitest coverage thresholds; `make ci-full` is wired to use
  `pnpm test:coverage` once `FLOTSAM_RUN_QUALITY_GATES=1`.
- Go coverage thresholds should be added as a follow-up once the preferred
  reporting format and enforcement target are chosen.
- Android and extension coverage thresholds should wait until those test suites
  are substantial enough to avoid meaningless numbers.

## Example Generic Stages

1. `setup`
   - install Go, Node, Java
   - install analyzer binaries
2. `policy`
   - `make ci-changed` or `make ci-full`
3. `quality`
   - full quality gates with `FLOTSAM_RUN_QUALITY_GATES=1`
4. `security`
   - Semgrep, gosec, govulncheck, gitleaks, Android lint, detekt
5. `artifacts`
   - publish coverage and analyzer output

## Notes

- The repo is not yet ready for strict enforcement across every surface.
- The scripts are intentionally audit-first until the existing backlog is
  reduced.
- Once the current governance debt is addressed, audit mode should be retired
  in CI.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Run full-repo policy and analyzer checks.
# Usage: run-full.sh
#
# Environment:
#   FLOTSAM_STRICT=1  — exit non-zero on any failure (default: advisory)
set -euo pipefail

repo_root="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"
cd "$repo_root"

STRICT="${FLOTSAM_STRICT:-0}"
EXIT_CODE=0

# --- Step 1: Risk classification (full repo) ---
echo "=== Risk Classification (full repo) ==="
python3 scripts/ci/detect-risk.py --all --severity-threshold critical || true
echo ""

# --- Step 2: Test standard ---
echo "=== Test Standard ==="
if ! make test-standard; then
    echo "test-standard FAILED"
    EXIT_CODE=1
fi
echo ""

# --- Step 3: Static analyzers ---
echo "=== Static Analyzers ==="
if ! bash scripts/ci/run-static-analyzers.sh; then
    echo "Static analysis reported findings"
    if [[ "$STRICT" == "1" ]]; then
        EXIT_CODE=1
    fi
fi
echo ""

# --- Step 4: Policy checks ---
echo "=== Policy Checks ==="
if ! bash scripts/ci/check-doc-commands.sh; then
    echo "Doc command audit found mismatches"
    if [[ "$STRICT" == "1" ]]; then
        EXIT_CODE=1
    fi
fi
if ! python3 scripts/ci/check-placeholders.py; then
    echo "Placeholder check found mismatches"
    if [[ "$STRICT" == "1" ]]; then
        EXIT_CODE=1
    fi
fi
echo ""

# --- Summary ---
if [[ "$EXIT_CODE" != "0" ]]; then
    if [[ "$STRICT" == "1" ]]; then
        echo "FAILED — strict mode, exiting with error."
    else
        echo "Issues found (advisory mode — not blocking)."
        EXIT_CODE=0
    fi
else
    echo "All checks passed."
fi

exit "$EXIT_CODE"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Run available static analyzers, skip gracefully if not installed.
# Exit code: 0 = no findings (or no tools installed), 1 = findings reported.
set -euo pipefail

repo_root="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"
cd "$repo_root"

FINDINGS=0
RAN=0

# --- gosec ---
if command -v gosec &>/dev/null; then
    echo "=== gosec ==="
    if (cd api && gosec ./...); then
        echo "gosec: clean"
    else
        echo "gosec: findings reported"
        FINDINGS=1
    fi
    RAN=1
    echo ""
else
    echo "gosec not installed — skipping"
fi

# --- govulncheck ---
if command -v govulncheck &>/dev/null; then
    echo "=== govulncheck ==="
    if (cd api && govulncheck ./...); then
        echo "govulncheck: clean"
    else
        echo "govulncheck: findings reported"
        FINDINGS=1
    fi
    RAN=1
    echo ""
else
    echo "govulncheck not installed — skipping"
fi

# --- semgrep ---
if command -v semgrep &>/dev/null; then
    echo "=== semgrep ==="
    if semgrep --config auto --quiet .; then
        echo "semgrep: clean"
    else
        echo "semgrep: findings reported"
        FINDINGS=1
    fi
    RAN=1
    echo ""
else
    echo "semgrep not installed — skipping"
fi

if [[ "$RAN" == "0" ]]; then
    echo "No static analyzers installed. Install gosec, govulncheck, or semgrep for enhanced checks."
fi

exit "$FINDINGS"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Run available static analyzers, skip gracefully if not installed.
# Exit code: 0 = no findings (or no tools installed), 1 = findings reported.
set -euo pipefail

repo_root="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"
cd "$repo_root"

FINDINGS=0
RAN=0

# --- gosec ---
if command -v gosec &>/dev/null; then
    echo "=== gosec ==="
    if (cd api && gosec ./...); then
        echo "gosec: clean"
    else
        echo "gosec: findings reported"
        FINDINGS=1
    fi
    RAN=1
    echo ""
else
    echo "gosec not installed — skipping"
fi

# --- govulncheck ---
if command -v govulncheck &>/dev/null; then
    echo "=== govulncheck ==="
    if (cd api && govulncheck ./...); then
        echo "govulncheck: clean"
    else
        echo "govulncheck: findings reported"
        FINDINGS=1
    fi
    RAN=1
    echo ""
else
    echo "govulncheck not installed — skipping"
fi

# --- semgrep ---
if command -v semgrep &>/dev/null; then
    echo "=== semgrep ==="
    if semgrep --config auto --quiet .; then
        echo "semgrep: clean"
    else
        echo "semgrep: findings reported"
        FINDINGS=1
    fi
    RAN=1
    echo ""
else
    echo "semgrep not installed — skipping"
fi

if [[ "$RAN" == "0" ]]; then
    echo "No static analyzers installed. Install gosec, govulncheck, or semgrep for enhanced checks."
fi

exit "$FINDINGS"

## Flotsam Monorepo Makefile — delegates to component Makefiles
## Usage: make <target>

# Load .env if present — makes vars available to shell commands in recipes
-include .env

.PHONY: api-build api-run api-generate api-test api-test-unit api-test-tdd \
        api-lint api-migrate-up api-migrate-down api-clean \
        web-install web-dev web-build web-lint web-clean web-test web-test-tdd web-schema-sync \
        lint \
        dev dev-db-up dev-db-down \
        test-quick test-standard test-full \
        ci-changed ci-full hooks-install \
        help

## api-build: Build all API binaries (flotsamd, flotsam-worker, flotsam)
api-build:
	$(MAKE) -C api build

## api-run: Run the API server (flotsamd)
api-run:
	$(MAKE) -C api run

## api-run-worker: Run the job worker (flotsam-worker --watch)
api-run-worker:
	$(MAKE) -C api run-worker

## api-generate: Run GraphQL code generation
api-generate:
	$(MAKE) -C api generate

## api-test: Run all API tests
api-test:
	$(MAKE) -C api test

## api-test-unit: Run API unit tests
api-test-unit:
	$(MAKE) -C api test-unit

## api-test-tdd: Run API tests with tdd-guard-go when installed
api-test-tdd:
	$(MAKE) -C api test-tdd

## api-lint: Run linter on the API
api-lint:
	$(MAKE) -C api lint

## api-migrate-up: Apply database migrations
api-migrate-up:
	$(MAKE) -C api migrate-up

## api-migrate-down: Roll back last database migration
api-migrate-down:
	$(MAKE) -C api migrate-down

## api-seed: Populate dev database with sample data
api-seed:
	$(MAKE) -C api seed

## api-dev-token: Mint a dev JWT for local testing
api-dev-token:
	$(MAKE) -C api dev-token

## api-clean: Remove API build artifacts
api-clean:
	$(MAKE) -C api clean

## web-install: Install web frontend dependencies
web-install:
	$(MAKE) -C web install

## web-dev: Start Vite dev server (port 8080, proxies /graphql → :8081)
web-dev:
	$(MAKE) -C web dev

## web-build: Type-check and build web frontend for production
web-build:
	$(MAKE) -C web build

## web-lint: Run ESLint on web frontend
web-lint:
	$(MAKE) -C web lint

## web-clean: Remove web build artifacts and node_modules
web-clean:
	$(MAKE) -C web clean

## web-test: Run web unit tests
web-test:
	$(MAKE) -C web test

## web-test-tdd: Run web tests with TDD Guard reporter when configured
web-test-tdd:
	$(MAKE) -C web test-tdd

## web-schema-sync: Regenerate web/schema.graphql and graphql-env.d.ts from API SDL files
web-schema-sync:
	cat api/graph/schema/*.graphql > web/schema.graphql
	cd web && pnpm exec gql-tada generate-output --tsconfig tsconfig.app.json

## lint: Run all linters (Go + web)
lint: api-lint web-lint

## dev: Start full-stack dev environment (Docker PG + air + Vite) with hot reload
dev:
	bash scripts/dev.sh

## dev-db-up: Start standalone dev database (PG 16 + pgvector + MinIO on port 5433)
dev-db-up:
	docker compose -f compose/db.yml up -d --wait
	@echo ""
	@echo "Dev database starting on port 5433. Use:"
	@echo "  DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/flotsam?sslmode=disable"
	@echo ""
	@echo "MinIO console: http://localhost:9001 (minioadmin/minioadmin)"

## dev-db-down: Stop and remove the standalone dev database container
dev-db-down:
	docker compose -f compose/db.yml down

## test-quick: Fast checks — Go unit tests, TS type-check + build, ESLint (~10-20s, no DB)
test-quick: api-test-unit web-build web-lint

## test-standard: Full local validation — quick + web unit tests + Go lint (~30-60s, no DB)
test-standard: test-quick web-test api-lint

## test-full: Complete validation — standard + integration tests (requires DB + running services)
test-full: test-standard api-test

## ci-changed: Run risk-aware checks on changed files (vs origin/main)
ci-changed:
	bash scripts/ci/run-changed.sh

## ci-full: Run full-repo policy and analyzer checks
ci-full:
	bash scripts/ci/run-full.sh

## hooks-install: Configure git to use .githooks/ for pre-commit and pre-push
hooks-install:
	bash scripts/install-hooks.sh

## help: List all available targets
help:
	@echo ""
	@echo "Usage: make <target>"
	@echo ""
	@grep -hE '^## [a-z]' $(MAKEFILE_LIST) | sed 's/## //' | sort -t: -k1,1 | \
		while IFS=: read -r target desc; do \
			printf "  %-24s %s\n" "$$target" "$$desc"; \
		done

        scan-secrets scan-deps \
        help

## api-clean: Remove API build artifacts
api-clean:
	$(MAKE) -C api clean

## web-install: Install web frontend dependencies
web-install:
	$(MAKE) -C web install

## web-dev: Start Vite dev server (port 8080, proxies /graphql → :8081)
web-dev:
	$(MAKE) -C web dev

## web-build: Type-check and build web frontend for production
web-build:
	$(MAKE) -C web build

## web-lint: Run ESLint on web frontend
web-lint:
	$(MAKE) -C web lint

## web-clean: Remove web build artifacts and node_modules
web-clean:
	$(MAKE) -C web clean

## web-test: Run web unit tests
web-test:
	$(MAKE) -C web test

## web-test-tdd: Run web tests with TDD Guard reporter when configured
web-test-tdd:
	$(MAKE) -C web test-tdd

## web-schema-sync: Regenerate web/schema.graphql and graphql-env.d.ts from API SDL files
web-schema-sync:
	cat api/graph/schema/*.graphql > web/schema.graphql
	cd web && pnpm exec gql-tada generate-output --tsconfig tsconfig.app.json

## lint: Run all linters (Go + web)
lint: api-lint web-lint

## dev: Start full-stack dev environment (Docker PG + air + Vite) with hot reload
dev:
	bash scripts/dev.sh

## dev-db-up: Start standalone dev database (PG 16 + pgvector + MinIO on port 5433)
dev-db-up:
	docker compose -f compose/db.yml up -d --wait
	@echo ""
	@echo "Dev database starting on port 5433. Use:"
	@echo "  DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/flotsam?sslmode=disable"
	@echo ""
	@echo "MinIO console: http://localhost:9001 (minioadmin/minioadmin)"

## dev-db-down: Stop and remove the standalone dev database container
dev-db-down:
	docker compose -f compose/db.yml down

## test-quick: Fast checks — Go unit tests, TS type-check + build, ESLint (~10-20s, no DB)
test-quick: api-test-unit web-build web-lint

## test-standard: Full local validation — quick + web unit tests + Go lint (~30-60s, no DB)
test-standard: test-quick web-test api-lint

## test-full: Complete validation — standard + integration tests (requires DB + running services)
test-full: test-standard api-test

## ci-changed: Run risk-aware checks on changed files (vs origin/main)
ci-changed:
	bash scripts/ci/run-changed.sh

## ci-full: Run full-repo policy and analyzer checks
ci-full:
	bash scripts/ci/run-full.sh

## hooks-install: Configure git to use .githooks/ for pre-commit and pre-push
hooks-install:
	bash scripts/install-hooks.sh

## help: List all available targets
help:
	@echo ""
	@echo "Usage: make <target>"
	@echo ""
	@grep -hE '^## [a-z]' $(MAKEFILE_LIST) | sed 's/## //' | sort -t: -k1,1 | \
		while IFS=: read -r target desc; do \
			printf "  %-24s %s\n" "$$target" "$$desc"; \
		done

input.content:
# Secret and Dependency Scanning

Two scanners are integrated into the CI pipeline via
`scripts/ci/run-static-analyzers.sh`. Both are optional — the script skips
gracefully when a tool is not installed.

## gitleaks — Secret Scanning

Detects hardcoded secrets (API keys, tokens, passwords) in the git history and
working tree.

### Install

```bash
# macOS
brew install gitleaks

# Go
go install github.com/gitleaks/gitleaks/v8@latest

# Binary releases
# https://github.com/gitleaks/gitleaks/releases
```

### Run

```bash
make scan-secrets             # standalone
make ci-full                  # runs as part of full CI
```

### CI artifacts

For SARIF output (GitHub Code Scanning, etc.):

```bash
gitleaks detect --source . --report-format sarif --report-path gitleaks.sarif
```

### Configuration

Place a `.gitleaks.toml` at the repo root to customize rules or add allowlists.
See [gitleaks docs](https://github.com/gitleaks/gitleaks#configuration) for
the config format.

---

## osv-scanner — Dependency Vulnerability Scanning

Checks project lockfiles against the OSV database for known vulnerabilities in
dependencies.

### Install

```bash
# macOS
brew install osv-scanner

# Go
go install github.com/google/osv-scanner/cmd/osv-scanner@latest

# Binary releases
# https://github.com/google/osv-scanner/releases
```

### Run

```bash
make scan-deps                # standalone
make ci-full                  # runs as part of full CI
```

The scanner checks:
- `api/go.sum` — Go dependencies
- `web/pnpm-lock.yaml` — Node.js dependencies

### CI artifacts

For JSON output:

```bash
osv-scanner scan --lockfile=api/go.sum --lockfile=web/pnpm-lock.yaml --format json > osv-report.json
```

---

## Integration

Both tools are called from `scripts/ci/run-static-analyzers.sh`, which is
invoked by `make ci-full` (via `scripts/ci/run-full.sh`). Findings are reported
but do not block unless `FLOTSAM_STRICT=1` is set.
